title: Daewoong acquires Turn Bio age-reversal assets — Korea's first major OSK reprogramming deal
description: Korean pharma Daewoong acquired Turn Biotechnologies' partial-epigenetic-reprogramming platform, marking Asia's first major M&A move into OSK reprogramming as a longevity modality. Combined with Life Biosciences' ER-100 first dosing in March, partial reprogramming is now actively backed by both venture and large pharma capital.

title: Tau-targeting drug slows early Alzheimer's progression — neurodegeneration aging axis advances
description: New tau-targeting therapy reported clinically meaningful slowing of early Alzheimer's progression, extending the field's pivot beyond amyloid to direct tau pathology — a key node in brain aging. Combined with AlzeCure's ACD856 Phase Ib completion and AB Science's masitinib ALS survival signal, the neurodegenerative aging pipeline is accelerating.

title: Repair Biotechnologies REP-0004 emerges as mRNA-LNP atherosclerosis-reversal candidate alongside Cyclarity
description: Fight Aging interview with Repair Biotechnologies positions REP-0004, an mRNA lipid-nanoparticle therapy aimed at reducing intracellular free cholesterol, alongside Cyclarity's UDP-003 7-ketocholesterol clearance — converging the atherosclerosis-as-aging-damage thesis on two distinct druggable mechanisms heading into 2H 2026.

title: Universal mammalian aging clocks published — opens screen for cross-species geroprotectors
description: Tyshkovskiy and Gladyshev (Harvard Medical) published universal biological aging markers shared across mammalian species — providing a framework to screen interventions in mice and rapidly translate findings to humans. The team explicitly framed the tool as a candidate-identification engine for lifespan-extending interventions.

title: Kirkland Foresight talk: 15 active senolytic trials including ALSENLITE, SToMP-AD, SENSURV
description: James Kirkland (Mayo) detailed 15 active senolytic clinical trials in the senolytic network alone — including ALSENLITE pilot, SToMP-AD multicenter Alzheimer's trial, NIH-funded SENSURV (D+Q vs F vs placebo) starting June, plus ONR osteoarthritis trial and multiple COVID complications studies. The pipeline is now broad enough to support indication-level safety/efficacy verdicts.

title: Daewoong acquires Turn Bio age-reversal assets
description: Korean pharma Daewoong acquired Turn Biotechnologies' partial-reprogramming platform and assets, expanding Asian pharma footprint in epigenetic age-reversal modalities for the first time at major M&A scale.

title: Tau-targeting drug slows early Alzheimer's progression
description: New clinical readout shows tau-targeting therapy meaningfully slowing early Alzheimer's progression, extending the neurodegeneration aging pipeline beyond amyloid-centric approaches.

title: Retro Biosciences $1.8B moment: hopes up as Alzheimer's protein-clearance trial advances
description: Coverage of Retro Biosciences' $1.8B valuation positions the company's autophagy-focused oral Alzheimer's trial, with no DLTs to date and August data expected, as the field's clearest pre-IPO catalyst.

title: 'The field has crossed a threshold of legitimacy' — longevity ecosystem maturation
description: Longevity.Technology editorial framing argues the field has crossed a threshold of mainstream legitimacy in 2026, citing convergence of FDA Reagan-Udall engagement, XPRIZE Healthspan progress, $8.5B 2024 capital, and structural pharma M&A interest.

title: Top 30 finalists named for longevity pitch competition
description: Longevity Investor Network announced top 30 finalists for its 2026 pitch competition, signaling deeper venture deal flow concentration around aging-focused startups across diagnostics, therapeutics, and digital health.

title: Longevity clinics face accountability era as consumer adoption scales
description: Longevity.Technology reports rising regulatory and consumer scrutiny of longevity clinics — paralleling Florida emerging as a US hotbed for bio-age reversal — and pressure on operators to substantiate biological-age and healthspan claims.

title: TreeFrog's 3D neural therapy targets Parkinson's decline
description: TreeFrog Therapeutics is advancing a 3D neural cell-therapy platform for Parkinson's, expanding cell-replacement approaches to neurodegeneration that intersect with brain-aging interventions.

title: Emagene.life, Adam Health launch men's AI longevity care
description: Emagene.life and Adam Health launched an AI-driven longevity-care platform targeting men's-health protocols, joining the wave of clinic-tech hybrids commercializing personalized geroscience interventions.

title: 'Universal' aging clocks offer new clues to longevity therapies
description: Tyshkovskiy and Gladyshev published universal biological aging markers shared across mammalian species — opening a framework for screening interventions in mice and rapidly translating to humans for candidate lifespan-extending therapies.

title: James Kirkland Foresight talk: 15 active senolytic trials underway
description: Kirkland (Mayo Clinic) details the active senolytic clinical network — 15 trials including ALSENLITE, SToMP-AD, SENSURV (D+Q vs F vs placebo starting June), ONR-funded osteoarthritis study, plus COVID-FIS / FISETIN / COVFIS-HOME complications trials. Cautions senolytics should currently only be used in controlled trials.

title: Repair Biotechnologies REP-0004: mRNA-LNP therapy to clear intracellular free cholesterol
description: Fight Aging interview details REP-0004 — Repair Biotechnologies' mRNA lipid-nanoparticle therapy designed to reduce excess intracellular free cholesterol, regress atherosclerotic plaque, and reverse fatty liver disease — adding a second damage-clearance modality alongside Cyclarity's UDP-003.

title: SIRT6 overexpression reverses age-related chromatin accessibility changes in mouse liver
description: New murine study finds SIRT6 overexpression reverses age-related chromatin accessibility changes in male liver, reinforcing the sirtuin pathway as a tractable epigenetic-aging target.

title: Retinal RetiAGE bio-age correlates with osteoporosis risk and fractures
description: Study finds retinal biological age measured by RetiAGE clock correlates with osteoporosis risk, bone mineral density, and fracture incidence — reinforcing the multi-system coherence of aging-damage clocks for clinical screening.

title: Aged CD8+ T cells and secreted factors drive hippocampal cognitive decline
description: Research shows aged circulating CD8+ T cells and their secreted factors directly contribute to hippocampal cognitive decline, extending the immune-aging-brain axis and reinforcing the case for adaptive-immune rejuvenation in dementia prevention.

title: Microglial senescence consolidated as Alzheimer's-driving aging mechanism
description: Fight Aging review consolidates evidence that microglial senescence drives Alzheimer's pathology and discusses senolytics plus microglial depletion-repopulation as the most promising near-term interventions.

title: PM2.5-attributable dementia deaths in China rose substantially 2000-2024
description: Study reports PM2.5-attributable dementia deaths in China increased substantially from 2000 to 2024, with population aging the dominant driver post-2013, strengthening the air-pollution-as-aging-amplifier evidence base.

title: WHO physical activity guidelines may underestimate optimal level
description: Epidemiological review finds the standard 150 min/week moderate-to-vigorous activity recommendation likely understates the optimum for cardiovascular protection, supporting upward revision of geroprotective exercise targets.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 hsCRP 83-86% knockdown — Phase 2 H1 2026
description: BioAge Labs reported clean Phase 1 for oral NLRP3 inhibitor BGE-102 with 87-93% of participants reaching hsCRP <2 mg/L; Phase 2 cardiovascular risk trial planned for H1 2026, data H2 2026.

title: Insilico + Human Longevity AI longevity foundation model collaboration
description: Insilico Medicine and Human Life Foundation Models (a Human Longevity subsidiary) signed a multi-million-dollar collaboration to build industry-first AI foundation models for longevity science, combining Insilico's Pharma.AI with HLI's multi-omic clinical data.

title: AstraZeneca tozorakimab Phase III MIRANDA COPD primary endpoint hit
description: AstraZeneca's anti-IL-33 antibody tozorakimab met its primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial, validating IL-33/ST2 as a druggable inflammaging axis in a major geriatric respiratory disease.

title: ADM consumer report: healthy aging supplements pivot to functional versatility + scientific credibility
description: ADM released a consumer report on healthy aging and longevity supplements arguing the winning path forward combines scientific credibility with functional versatility and consumer-centric design — signaling supplement-industry consolidation around evidence-based positioning.

title: American Aging Association AGE 2026 session: aging science meets real-world application
description: AGE 2026 Session 4 focuses on bridging aging science and real-world clinical/consumer application, reflecting the field's accelerating translation push alongside Vitalist Bay-style conference programming.

title: Sun Sentinel: Florida emerges as US hub for bio-age-reversal clinics
description: Sun Sentinel reports Florida has become a major US center for clinics offering biological age slow-down and reversal interventions, paralleling rapid consumer longevity infrastructure scale.

title: Common diabetes drug linked to 'exceptional longevity' in women
description: Observational analysis links a commonly prescribed diabetes drug with substantially elevated odds of reaching exceptional old age in women, reinforcing metformin-class agents as candidate geroprotectors.

title: KHL Foundation pushes rejuvenation gene therapy packages for over-60s
description: Ken Scott / Helga Sands KHL Foundation is offering an open-access combination gene-therapy package for adults over 60 — a controversial first push to bring rejuvenation gene therapies directly to consumers ahead of clinical validation.
